heading: ## Sample size calculation
body:
- **Formula:** n = (1.96 + 0.84)² · σ² / δ²
- **Parameters:** α = 0.05 (two-sided), power = 0.80, δ = 5.4 (MCID for PAM-13), σ = 10 (based on Hibbard 2004, PMID 15333167, SD range 9–11)
- **Calculation:** n = (2.80)² · 100 / 29.16 = 7.84 · 100 / 29.16 ≈ 26.9 per group; adjusted for 20% attrition: n ≥ 34 per group; final target: N ≥ 55 per group (conservative overshoot to account for clustering and stratification)
- **Reference:** Hibbard JH, et al. Development of the Patient Activation Measure (PAM): conceptualizing and measuring activation in patients and consumers. Health Serv Res. 2004;39(4 Pt 1):1005-1026. PMID: 15333167.

heading: ## Risk matrix
body:
| Risk | Probability | Impact | Mitigation |
|------|-------------|--------|------------|
| Low recruitment (N < 55 per group) | Medium | High | Multi-site recruitment (3+ sites), extended enrollment window, adaptive sample size re-estimation at interim |
| High attrition (>20%) | Medium | High | Retention strategies (reminders, incentives, flexible scheduling), per-protocol sensitivity analysis |
| PAM-13 ceiling effect in activated patients | Low | Medium | Stratify by baseline activation level, use alternative outcome (e.g., EQ-5D-5L) as sensitivity |
| AI system technical failure (downtime >48h) | Low | High | Redundant deployment (cloud + local), manual backup protocol, DSMB oversight |
| Contamination between arms (control exposed to AI) | Medium | Medium | Cluster randomization by site, monitoring of control arm AI usage, per-protocol analysis excluding contaminated clusters |
| Unblinding of outcome assessors | Low | Low | Automated PAM-13 administration via tablet, assessor blinding to allocation, independent data entry |

heading: ## Limitations
body:
- Single-blinded design (patients and clinicians cannot be blinded to AI intervention)
- Reliance on self-reported PAM-13 (subject to social desirability bias and ceiling effects)
- Short follow-up (6 months) limits assessment of long-term activation maintenance
- Sample size calculated for primary endpoint only; secondary analyses may be underpowered
- Generalizability limited to outpatient chronic disease populations in high-resource settings
- AI system performance may degrade with out-of-distribution patient populations
- No active comparator (control is usual care, not alternative AI or human coaching)
- Pre-registration placeholder not yet replaced with actual OSF identifier
